_meta.description: Structured seed data for Cluster Study D3 build. Hub nodes = G7 Tier 1 clients (floor-state: no ring, no relevance decay — these are established relationships, not live signals). Adjacent nodes = competitor/analogous brands identified via manual research bootstrap, pending Signal Stacks maturity.

confidence_key.established: Drawn from stable category knowledge, not independently verified this session — spot-check before using in outreach

_meta.vertical_notes: RESOLVED 2026-07-11 (Part II, II.10 open question #1): the original 6 verticals were single-assignment categorical labels; the single 'vertical' field on each hub node has been removed and replaced with a 'business_verticals' object (0-5 weighted score per well, mirroring 'cultural_verticals'). Expanded from 6 to 9 wells this pass -- added beauty_retail and media_social_platforms to accommodate signal-sourced entities (Ulta Beauty, Reddit) that didn't fit the original 6, and added entertainment per the plan doc's own Liquid Death hybrid-brand example (Part II, II.1). cpgs and sports, previously empty, now have partial coverage (Mojo Energy Pouches scores 4 on cpgs; PLL scores 5 on sports) -- still worth broadening with future roster additions.

ring_types.signal: teal — unified ring for Signal Stacks detection and RFP-sourced events (v1 build only; re-evaluate under Map Engine v2's white/gray glow model)

_meta.new_addition_treatment: pulsing glow (brighter + slow pulse) for first week, not a separate ring type
_meta.map_engine_v2_status: Planned as a separate v2 branch/rebuild. See G7_Cluster_Study_Plan.md Part II. Cultural verticals list (11, subject to revision): music_performance, sports, gaming, comedy, festival, talent_celebrity_partnerships, film_tv_inclusion, college_marketing, outdoor_adventure, inter_brand_collaborations, creator_influencer_partnerships, wellness_fitness. Business verticals list needs revisiting as weighted wells rather than single-assignment categories. Hub nodes do NOT yet have cultural_verticals or re-scored business_verticals fields — that data entry is future work once the v2 branch starts.
_meta.jackson_hole_flag: Resolved — Jackson Hole Mountain Resort (JHMR) is now a corporate_parents entry; Rendezvous Music Festival is the scored hub node (id renamed from 'jackson_hole').
_meta.cultural_verticals_note: Applied from user FPO sheet 2026-07-11. business_verticals migration (Phase 1, Part II.1) not yet reflected here — still separate follow-up work.
_meta.vertical_notes_update: Film/TV and Inclusion confirmed as two separate cultural verticals (13 wells total, not merged). Existing 'film_tv_inclusion' scores were split: renamed to 'film_tv' (score preserved), with a new 'inclusion' field. RESOLVED 2026-07-11: all 19 entities now have an Inclusion score (two user-provided FPO batches). One data gap surfaced during this pass and was fixed separately: rendezvous_music_festival had no cultural_verticals object at all (missed by both the original 14-entity and later 4-new-entity FPO rounds) -- it now has the full 13-well object with Inclusion (2) populated and the remaining 12 wells null, still needing a scoring pass. Mojo Energy Pouches' Inclusion was set to 1 (no evidence found) -- its parent/operating-entity structure (Swisher subsidiary) remains a separate unresolved question, flagged on the node itself and in corporate_parents.
_meta.business_verticals_source: Claude first-pass draft across all 19 hub nodes, reviewed and corrected by user 2026-07-11. See vertical_notes for taxonomy-expansion rationale. Scores are a first pass built from category descriptions, not deep per-entity research -- most entities score high on exactly one well; real cross-vertical research (the kind the cultural pass received) is future work, not done here.
_meta.rendezvous_cultural_gap_resolved: RESOLVED 2026-07-11: rendezvous_music_festival now has a complete 13-well cultural_verticals object (previously missing entirely -- see vertical_notes_update). All 19 hub nodes now have complete cultural_verticals scoring.
_meta.inclusion_scale_resolved: RESOLVED 2026-07-11: the four half-point Inclusion scores (Atlassian, Workday, Reddit, PLL -- originally 4.5) were rounded down to 4 to keep the integer 0-5 scale consistent across cultural_verticals and business_verticals. Cisco and Ulta Beauty's Inclusion scores of 5 were already whole numbers and are unaffected.

hub_nodes.name: White Claw

adjacent.name: Truly
adjacent.note: Category leader but losing share; White Claw now >60% of segment dollar sales

adjacent.name: Nütrl
adjacent.note: Vodka-based, rated well in taste tests

adjacent.name: High Noon
adjacent.note: Vodka seltzer, frequently outperforms White Claw/Truly in taste comparisons

adjacent.name: Surfside
adjacent.note: Non-carbonated spiked drink pulling Gen Z attention from hard seltzer

adjacent.name: Sun Cruiser
adjacent.note: Boston Beer's direct answer to Surfside; growing faster than Surfside currently

g7_notes.case_study_learnings: Audience is receptive to data capture if incentivized with exclusive experiences or merch. Simpler tactics that ladder to immediate gratification outperform deep concepts and complicated consumer journeys.
g7_notes.relationship_notes: Part of the Mark Anthony Brands relationship — G7's warmest client relationship overall. Historically included talent booking; that has recently changed (Experiential only going forward).

hub_nodes.name: TurboTax / Intuit
hub_nodes.category: Consumer tax prep / personal finance software

g7_notes.case_study_learnings: Consumers must be strongly incentivized to engage. Effective pattern: load the experience with incentives and directly link TurboTax employees with consumers for follow-up after the event. Has partnered with talent on economic-fluency-themed initiatives.
g7_notes.relationship_notes: Lapsed — last worked together roughly a year ago, not a current client. G7 has done both Experiential and Talent Booking work for them, at both the TurboTax and Intuit corporate level.

hub_nodes.name: Subaru

adjacent.name: REI
adjacent.note: Outdoor-lifestyle audience overlap, longstanding Subaru marketing territory

g7_notes.case_study_learnings: Top KPI is hot-lead capture. Secondary goal: impacting the Overlanding space currently dominated by Jeep. Audience is highly loyal, responds well to exclusive programming, and will go out of its way to engage.
g7_notes.relationship_notes: Current client. G7 has pitched for additional Experiential business without success, and separately pitched Talent Booking business that also did not close (RFP: Lost, Talent lane) — worth surfacing as a lookalike-search trigger per Section 6.

g7_notes.case_study_learnings: G7 manages the Rendezvous Music Festival end-to-end (Experiential + Talent Booking). Attendance has trended down in recent years since the festival shifted from free to paid admission.
g7_notes.relationship_notes: Yearly client; work is expected to continue.
g7_notes.parent_note: Parent entity is Jackson Hole Mountain Resort (JHMR) — the festival is G7's actual client program; JHMR itself is the hosting resort, not directly scored.

corporate_parents.name: Mark Anthony Brands, Inc.
corporate_parents.note: G7's warmest overall relationship. G7 runs experiential programs for White Claw, Mike's Hard Lemonade, Cayman Jack, Ole, and others under this parent. Talent booking was previously part of the relationship but has recently ended.
corporate_parents.flag: Resolved — Mike's Hard Lemonade, Cayman Jack, and Ole have been promoted to full hub nodes with their own competitor research. Their zone (experiential) is an assumption based on the MABI-wide shift away from talent booking — confirm with the team, since White Claw itself remains zoned Combo.

corporate_parents.name: Intuit
corporate_parents.note: G7 has worked with Intuit at the corporate level as well as with TurboTax specifically — both are real G7 relationships, not just a brand/parent naming distinction.

corporate_parents.name: Swisher
corporate_parents.note: G7 works with Mojo Energy, a Swisher child brand. Swisher has a variety of other children not yet detailed.
corporate_parents.flag: Swisher's other child brands are not yet identified — worth a follow-up pass once there's time, similar to how the MABI family was expanded above.

corporate_parents.name: Jackson Hole Mountain Resort (JHMR)
corporate_parents.note: G7 runs the Rendezvous Music Festival for JHMR end-to-end (Experiential + Talent Booking). JHMR is the parent/hosting resort; the festival itself is the actual G7 client program and the scored hub node.

structural_analogs.note: Not competitors and not audience-analogous brands — these are two multi-brand parent clients where G7's work looks similar across children. Useful for understanding how to structure/pitch multi-brand parent relationships, not for lookalike-brand outreach. This is a distinct relationship type from direct_competitor and analogous_audience, tracked at the corporate_parents level rather than between individual hub nodes.
